summary:
To ensure patient safety by observing adverse effects and determining a safe dosage range. Agent1 reasoned based solely on ensuring patients are protected from harm during early drug testing phases through careful monitoring for side-effects and establishing dosing limits safely. Key focus was placed only on identifying potential risks before broader use can be considered clinically viable or ethically sound given current medical standards regarding informed consent protections at this stage of development where efficacy has not yet been established beyond initial promising results warranting further investigation into human subjects' responses specifically under controlled conditions designed explicitly around minimizing hazards while gathering necessary data about pharmacokinetics etc... 

-Agent2 will now provide its summary focusing strictly within those constraints provided initially without deviation towards additional context outside what’s required per instructions.* Safety concerns take precedence over other considerations such as effectiveness since these studies primarily seek out how new treatments affect humans rather than proving their utility against disease.*
To determine if any dose level causes harmful reactions; establish maximum tolerated doses which define upper boundaries needed when prescribing medication later stages once proven effective enough via subsequent trial designs aimed more broadly toward demonstrating therapeutic benefit across larger populations after confirming basic biological plausibility here first ensures public health interests remain paramount throughout entire research pipeline process leading up full approval status granted regulatory bodies like FDA post successful completion all requisite milestones including rigorous statistical analyses supporting claims made along way.*

formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): To ensure patient safety by observing adverse effects and determining a safe dosage range. Agent1 reasoned based solely on ensuring patients are protected from harm during early drug testing phases through careful monitoring for side-effects and establishing dosing limits safely. Key focus was placed only on identifying potential risks before broader use can be considered clinically viable or ethically sound given current medical standards regarding informed consent protections at this stage of development where efficacy has not yet been established beyond initial promising results warranting further investigation into human subjects' responses specifically under controlled conditions designed explicitly around minimizing hazards while gathering necessary data about pharmacokinetics etc... 

-Agent2 will now provide its summary focusing strictly within those constraints provided initially without deviation towards additional context outside what’s required per instructions.* Safety concerns take precedence over other considerations such as effectiveness since these studies primarily seek out how new treatments affect humans rather than proving their utility against disease.*
To determine if any dose level causes harmful reactions; establish maximum tolerated doses which define upper boundaries needed when prescribing medication later stages once proven effective enough via subsequent trial designs aimed more broadly toward demonstrating therapeutic benefit across larger populations after confirming basic biological plausibility here first ensures public health interests remain paramount throughout entire research pipeline process leading up full approval status granted regulatory bodies like FDA post successful completion all requisite milestones including rigorous statistical analyses supporting claims made along way.*
